rationale: The citing paper uses Berger and Milkman (2012) for a specific conceptual role: to justify why emotion should be measured when analyzing information diffusion. In the text-processing section, it cites this work (with a small set of related studies) as evidence that emotional content drives virality, which motivates inclusion of positive/negative emotion features in the empirical design. Although the citation is not central to identification or core causal claims, it meaningfully informs the paper's framing and variable construction rather than serving as a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Berger and Milkman's insight that emotional content increases social transmission to motivate emotion-based text features in its analysis of hype news diffusion in health care.
